findings.title: partitionClosed uses wrong date field: rejected_at checked before accepted_at, but neither field exists in the schema
findings.detail: partitionClosed reads `app.rejected_at || app.accepted_at || app.updated_at`. The DB schema tracks terminal-stage timestamps via the single `closed_at` field (see timeline.js line 31: `application.closed_at`). If `rejected_at` and `accepted_at` are null for all records, every card falls back to `updated_at`, which is a different semantics (last edit, not stage close date). The partition threshold will be wrong for apps edited after closure. checklist item 'Older items (â‰Ą14 days) collapsed' will appear to pass during a short test session because all cards are fresh, but will silently use the wrong date in production for apps that were updated after rejection.
findings.suggested_fix: Use `app.closed_at || app.updated_at` to match the timeline.js convention, or verify what fields the API actually returns and document the chosen field in a comment.

findings.title: Dragging a card to the Closed column silently assigns 'rejected' even for cards dragged from 'accepted' â€” not tested in checklist
findings.detail: onClosedChange only fires emit('status-change', app.id, 'rejected') if the card is not already terminal. But an already-accepted card dragged within the Closed column (e.g. from recentClosed to olderClosed) will not change status (the guard `!TERMINAL_STAGES.includes(app.status)` prevents it) â€” that part is fine. However an active card dragged to Closed always becomes 'rejected', including offer-stage cards. The checklist does not include: 'Drag an offer-stage card to Closed â€” verify it becomes rejected, not accepted'. A user might intend 'accepted' for an offer card. The plan documents this decision explicitly (Step 5 item 3), but the checklist should include a test for this specific case so the conscious decision is validated in smoke testing, not silently assumed.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a checklist item: 'Drag an offer-stage card to Closed â€” confirm it moves to rejected (not accepted), and that the ghost header pulses if showClosed=false.'

findings.title: pulseGhost timeout is never cleared on component unmount â€” memory leak
findings.detail: `pulseTimeout` is a module-level `let` variable and is cleared on re-trigger, but there is no `onUnmounted` hook to clear it if App.vue is ever destroyed while the timeout is pending. For a single-page app where App.vue lives for the full session this is harmless in practice, but Vue recommends cleanup. More importantly, if the component were ever unmounted (test harness, future SSR) the timeout callback would set `pulseGhost.value` on a dead ref, potentially causing a Vue warning. The checklist has no item covering rapid repeated drags that retrigger the pulse before the 1500ms expires.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `onUnmounted(() => { if (pulseTimeout) clearTimeout(pulseTimeout) })` in App.vue. Add checklist item: 'Drag two cards to Closed in quick succession â€” confirm pulse fires for the second drag, no console errors.'

findings.title: showOlder (older items toggle) resets on every prop update, not just page load â€” undocumented behaviour
findings.detail: The plan says showOlder is ephemeral and resets on page load. But `showOlder` is a component-local ref initialised to false. Every time props.applications changes (which happens after every status-change API call), the `watch` runs and rebuilds `recentClosed` and `olderClosed`. If the user has expanded 'Show N older' and then any status change fires (even in a different card), the older section does NOT collapse â€” but its item list is rebuilt, which could reorder items. This is subtle but the checklist has no item: 'Expand older items, move an active card to a different active stage, verify older section stays expanded and does not reorder unexpectedly.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add checklist item: 'Expand the older items section, then move an active card â€” verify the older section stays open and items do not jump.' Consider whether the sort order of recentClosed / olderClosed should be deterministic (e.g. by closed_at desc).

findings.title: recentClosed / olderClosed draggable lists are separate from columns.accepted and columns.rejected â€” dual source of truth allows desync
findings.detail: The watch rebuilds both `columns.accepted/rejected` AND `recentClosed/olderClosed` from props. But vuedraggable mutates these arrays in place when a user drags a card. After a drag: (1) the dragged card is removed from `recentClosed` or `olderClosed` by vuedraggable, (2) the status-change emit triggers an API call and then refreshes applications, (3) the watch fires again and rebuilds from the server response. Between steps 2 and 3 there is a brief moment where columns.accepted/rejected and recentClosed/olderClosed are out of sync. More critically, if an active card is dragged from an active column into recentClosed, vuedraggable adds it to `recentClosed.value` immediately â€” `onClosedChange` emits the status change â€” but until the refresh completes, the card is in `recentClosed` with its old non-terminal status. The checklist does not cover: 'Drag card to Closed, verify card shows correctly (no duplicate, no wrong status) before and after the server round-trip.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add checklist item: 'Drag a card from Applied to Closed â€” verify it appears once in the Closed column immediately, and that after the page data refreshes it remains there with the rejected status (no duplicate/ghost).' Consider whether recentClosed/olderClosed should be derived computed from columns.accepted + columns.rejected rather than separate watchers.

findings.title: hover:text-ink on quiet company name paragraph has no effect â€” class applied to p element, not text node
findings.detail: The quiet company name paragraph has `['font-semibold font-condensed text-sm truncate', quiet ? 'text-ink-3' : 'text-ink', 'hover:text-ink transition-colors duration-200']`. Tailwind's `hover:text-ink` applies only when the p itself is hovered. Since the card's root div is the interactive element (role=button, click handler), the hover state on the p will actually work when the user hovers the card because CSS hover applies to any element under the cursor. However the plan says 'on hover, restore full text colour' â€” this is tested visually but the checklist does not include: 'Hover a quieted rejected card â€” verify company name text visually transitions to full-ink colour'. Without an explicit visual check this subtle intent can be missed in smoke testing.
findings.suggested_fix: Add checklist item: 'Hover a rejected card in the Closed column â€” confirm company name text brightens to full ink colour during hover (not just on focus).' Optionally use group-hover instead of hover: on the p, applied from the card root div, for more reliable intent.

findings.title: showUser paragraph in KanbanCard: quiet ? 'text-ink-3' : 'text-ink-3' â€” no-op branch produces dead code
findings.detail: `:class="['text-xs truncate mt-0.5', quiet ? 'text-ink-3' : 'text-ink-3']"` â€” both branches produce 'text-ink-3'. Same dead-code pattern appears in TimelineView.vue line 57 for role_title: `quiet ? 'text-ink-3' : 'text-ink-3'`. This is harmless at runtime but is evidence that the conditional was copy-pasted without checking the fallback value, which means the intent (what colour should non-quiet user email render at?) is unverified by the checklist.
findings.suggested_fix: Fix both to use the intended non-quiet token (likely 'text-ink-3' is correct for user email since it was always muted, but confirm). The role_title in TimelineView should probably be 'text-ink-3' in both cases â€” confirm and simplify to a static class. Add lint rule or checklist note to catch ternaries with identical branches.

findings.title: Mobile handleToggle scrollIntoView fires before showClosed DOM update â€” closedGroupRef may be null
findings.detail: handleToggle emits 'toggle-show-closed' and then awaits nextTick before calling scrollIntoView. But 'toggle-show-closed' is handled in App.vue (toggleShowClosed), which updates showClosed.value. That update propagates back to KanbanBoard as a prop change, which triggers a re-render. A single nextTick after the emit may not be sufficient for the Closed group element to be mounted and closedGroupRef to be populated â€” prop propagation + template re-render can require two ticks in Vue 3. If closedGroupRef is null the scroll is silently skipped (the ?. handles it), but the user sees no scroll. The checklist item 'Kanban (mobile): tap reveals Closed group and auto-scrolls' would catch this only if tested on a real device, but it is easy to miss in desktop DevTools responsive mode.
findings.suggested_fix: Add checklist item: 'On a real mobile device (or accurate iOS Safari simulation), tap the closed banner â€” verify the Closed group scrolls into view.' Consider using `await nextTick(); await nextTick()` or a `watch(closedGroupRef, ...)` approach to defer scroll until the ref is bound.

findings.title: Table empty-state button not keyboard-accessible from within the table â€” tab order and focus management not tested
findings.detail: The 'show N closed' button in the empty-state <td> is a native <button> so it is keyboard-reachable. But when the table switches from non-empty to empty (user hides all closed apps), keyboard focus is left on whatever element was previously focused â€” potentially on a row that no longer exists in the DOM. The checklist item 'Keyboard: toggles reachable by Tab and activate on Enter/Space' does not specify focus management when the list empties. A screen reader user who hid all apps will need to navigate from scratch to find the empty-state button.
findings.suggested_fix: Add checklist item: 'With keyboard only, hide all closed apps from Table view â€” verify focus moves to the empty-state button or a known landmark, not lost in the void.'

findings.title: Checklist missing: switching views while showClosed=false â€” ghost header state not preserved across view transitions
findings.detail: showClosed is global state in App.vue so it persists across view switches. But the checklist does not include: 'Set showClosed=false in Kanban, switch to Table, switch to Timeline, switch back to Kanban â€” verify ghost header is still showing and closedCount is correct in all three views.' Without this scenario, a bug where one view's toggle-show-closed event handler changes state in an unexpected way could go undetected.
findings.suggested_fix: Add checklist item: 'Set showClosed=false, switch between all three views (Kanban â†’ Table â†’ Timeline â†’ Kanban) â€” verify the hidden state is consistent and no view resets showClosed to true.'

findings.title: Checklist missing: localStorage key conflict / SSR-like scenario (private browsing, storage blocked)
findings.detail: `localStorage.getItem` and `localStorage.setItem` are called directly without try/catch. In Safari private browsing mode or when storage is blocked by browser policy, these throw a SecurityError. App.vue will crash at module initialization (the `const _stored = ...` line runs at setup time). The checklist covers persistence but not the storage-unavailable case.
findings.suggested_fix: Add checklist item: 'Open app in Safari private browsing (localStorage disabled) â€” verify app loads without error, showClosed defaults to true.' Wrap localStorage calls in try/catch with graceful fallback.

findings.title: Checklist missing: panelApp set to a closed app, then app is deleted (not just hidden) â€” panel coherence only tested for hide, not delete
findings.detail: The watcher closes the panel when showClosed is toggled to false while a terminal-stage app is open. But if the user deletes a closed app while its panel is open, or if the background data refresh removes it from the applications list, panelApp might point to a stale record. This is pre-existing behaviour but the new watcher's presence may give reviewers false confidence that all panel-coherence cases are handled. The checklist item 'Selecting a closed app then toggling to hide â†’ detail panel closes cleanly' only covers the toggle path.
findings.suggested_fix: Annotate in the checklist that the watcher only handles the hide-toggle case; deletion-while-open is a pre-existing gap. Out of scope for this PR, but note it explicitly.

findings.title: Checklist missing: KanbanBoard desktop drop zone for Closed column when showClosed=false â€” draggable v-show target is untested
findings.detail: The plan explicitly requires the drop zone to remain mounted via v-show (not v-if) so dragging to the hidden column still works. The implementation uses `v-show="showClosed"` on the drop target div. The checklist item 'Drag card to Closed while hidden â€” card disappears; ghost header pulses' covers the outcome but not the mechanism. Specifically: does vuedraggable fire the @change event on a v-show=false list? This is a known edge case in vuedraggable â€” some versions do not fire events on hidden drop targets. The checklist should explicitly verify the drag-to-hidden behaviour works (not just that the ghost pulses, but that the status-change API call fires).
findings.suggested_fix: Add checklist item: 'With showClosed=false, drag a card to the ghost column header area â€” open browser devtools Network tab and confirm a PATCH /api/applications/:id/status request fires with status=rejected.'

findings.title: Checklist missing: accepted app quieting â€” plan specifies accepted renders at full weight, code only quiets rejected
findings.detail: KanbanCard receives `:quiet="element.status === 'rejected'"` â€” correct per the plan (only rejected is quieted, accepted is full weight). But the checklist item 'closed cards are quieted (muted text)' does not distinguish accepted from rejected. A tester reading the checklist might quiet-check an accepted card and falsely fail, or skip checking that accepted cards render at full weight. Similarly TimelineView.vue line 58 passes `app.status === 'rejected'` to segmentStyle â€” accepted segments get the same grayscale filter as rejected. But the plan says 'Accepted timeline segments render at full weight.' The code is consistent for KanbanCard but TimelineView needs verification.
findings.suggested_fix: Add checklist items: 'Kanban: accepted card renders at full text weight (not quieted)', 'Timeline: accepted application bars render in full colour (not grayscale).' Verify TimelineView segmentStyle: the `isRejected` parameter currently also applies to all terminal stages â€” confirm it is only triggered by `app.status === 'rejected'`.

findings.title: Checklist missing: rapid toggle (hide/show in quick succession) â€” pulseGhost clearTimeout race not visually verified
findings.detail: triggerGhostPulse clears the previous timeout on re-entry â€” the reset logic is correct. But no checklist item exercises: 'Toggle showClosed off, drag a card to Closed (triggering pulse), then toggle showClosed on before the 1500ms expires â€” verify no lingering pulse class or visual artifact when the column re-opens.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add checklist item: 'While ghost header is pulsing (card just moved to Closed), click the toggle to show Closed â€” confirm pulse animation does not carry over to the visible column header.'

findings.title: Checklist missing: Timeline quieting uses CSS filter (grayscale+brightness) â€” reduced-motion preference does not gate CSS filters
findings.detail: segmentStyle applies `filter: grayscale(0.4) brightness(0.85)` for rejected apps. CSS filter is not a motion property and is not gated by prefers-reduced-motion â€” this is fine. However the plan says 'use a muted colour token on the timeline segment/line for rejected entries, consistent with the kanban card treatment â€” do not use opacity reduction'. The implementation uses a CSS filter instead of a colour token, which is a deviation from the spec. A CSS filter affects the entire painted segment including any hover effects. The checklist item 'Timeline: quieting behaviour' does not specify that the implementation must use colour tokens (not filters), so this deviation would pass the checklist.
findings.suggested_fix: Add checklist item: 'Timeline: rejected application bars appear visually muted â€” confirm they use colour/opacity approach consistent with Kanban text quieting, not a filter that could interfere with hover states.' Assess whether switching to a Tailwind CSS custom property approach is needed to match the plan's design intent.

residual_risks: The drag-to-hidden drop zone behaviour (T13) is the highest-risk unverified scenario. If vuedraggable does not fire @change on a v-show=false container, the entire 'drag to Closed while hidden' feature silently fails with no error â€” the card visually disappears from vuedraggable's active list but no status change is persisted.

The partitionClosed date field mismatch (T01) means the 14-day partition threshold is evaluated against updated_at (last edit) instead of the actual close date. Apps edited after rejection will appear in 'recent' indefinitely, defeating the purpose of time-based collapsing.
localStorage-unavailable crash (T11) is a real production risk for Safari private browsing users â€” self-hosters often use private tabs for privacy.

The dual source of truth between columns.accepted/rejected and recentClosed/olderClosed (T05) creates a brief inconsistency window during every status change. If API calls are slow (mobile connection), a user may see a card in both places or in neither place momentarily.
testing_gaps: No test for drag-to-hidden Closed column confirming the PATCH API call fires (the checklist verifies visual outcome only).
No test distinguishing accepted vs rejected quieting in Kanban and Timeline â€” checklist item 'closed cards quieted' conflates both.
No test for localStorage-unavailable failure mode.
No test for view switching while showClosed=false (cross-view state persistence).
No test for the 'Show N older' toggle surviving a background data refresh (showOlder state after any status change).
No test for rapid re-triggering of the ghost pulse (drag two cards in quick succession).
No test for the pulse animation clearing correctly when showClosed is toggled back to true mid-pulse.
No test for mobile scroll-to-Closed on a real device (nextTick sufficiency is device-dependent).
No test confirming network request fires (not just UI response) for drag-to-Closed.

The checklist item for screen reader state announcement does not specify which ARIA attributes to verify (aria-pressed value changes, aria-live announcement content) â€” too vague to be actionable without explicit attribute checks.
